title: Exercises: Resource Management for Quantum Computing

exercises.title: Implement a QPU Allocator with Multiple Strategies
exercises.description: Create a QPU allocator that supports multiple allocation strategies and can dynamically switch between them based on workload.
tasks: Define QPUProperties and QPUState dataclasses to represent QPU characteristics
Implement the QPUAllocator class with strategy selection
Create at least three allocation strategies: round-robin, least-loaded, and best-fit
Add QPU registration and state update methods
Implement allocation history tracking for analysis
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
from enum import Enum
import time

class AllocationStrategy(Enum):
    ROUND_ROBIN = "round_robin"
    LEAST_LOADED = "least_loaded"
    BEST_FIT = "best_fit"

@dataclass
class QPUProperties:
    qpu_id: str
    num_qubits: int
    fidelity: float
    cost_per_shot: float

@dataclass
class QPUState:
    properties: QPUProperties
    available: bool = True
    current_load: int = 0
    queue_time: float = 0.0

@dataclass
class JobRequirements:
    job_id: str
    min_qubits: int
    min_fidelity: float
    shots: int

class QPUAllocator:
    """Allocates QPUs to jobs using configurable strategies."""
    
    def __init__(self, strategy: AllocationStrategy):
        self.strategy = strategy
        self.qpus: Dict[str, QPUState] = {}
        self.allocation_history: List[Dict] = []
        self._rr_index = 0
    
    def register_qpu(self, state: QPUState):
        """Register a QPU."""
        # TODO: Implement
        pass
    
    def update_qpu(self, qpu_id: str, **kwargs):
        """Update QPU state."""
        # TODO: Implement
        pass
    
    def allocate(self, requirements: JobRequirements) -> Optional[str]:
        """Allocate a QPU for the job."""
        # TODO: Get suitable QPUs and apply strategy
        pass
    
    def _get_suitable_qpus(self, requirements: JobRequirements) -> List[QPUState]:
        """Filter QPUs that meet requirements."""
        # TODO: Implement filtering
        pass
    
    def _round_robin(self, qpus: List[QPUState]) -> str:
        """Round-robin allocation."""
        # TODO: Implement
        pass
    
    def _least_loaded(self, qpus: List[QPUState]) -> str:
        """Least-loaded allocation."""
        # TODO: Implement
        pass
    
    def _best_fit(self, qpus: List[QPUState], req: JobRequirements) -> str:
        """Best-fit allocation."""

        pass
    
    def get_statistics(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:

exercises.expectedOutput: A flexible QPU allocator that applies different strategies based on configuration
hints: Use defaultdict for counting allocations per QPU
The best-fit score should balance multiple factors
Remember to increment current_load after allocation
Suitable QPUs must pass all requirement filters

testCases.expectedBehavior: Each QPU receives approximately equal allocations
QPU with lowest load is selected

exercises.title: Build a Priority Job Queue
exercises.description: Create a priority queue system for quantum jobs with fair scheduling and starvation prevention.
tasks: Define PriorityLevel enum and QueuedJob dataclass
Implement QuantumJobQueue with multiple priority levels
Add submit, get_next, and cancel operations
Implement starvation prevention that promotes old jobs
Add queue statistics and visualization
exercises.expectedOutput: A priority queue with fair scheduling and statistics tracking
hints: Use heapq for efficient priority queue operations
The __lt__ method enables heap comparison between jobs
Promoted jobs need to be re-added to the index with new priority
Sort jobs when calculating position for accurate results

exercises.title: Create a Cost Optimizer
exercises.description: Build a cost optimizer that analyzes quantum workloads and suggests strategies for reducing execution costs.
tasks: Define CostModel dataclass for backend pricing
Implement cost estimation with volume discounts
Create optimization suggestion engine
Add batch planning for multiple jobs
Implement cost comparison across backends
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, List, Any, Optional
from enum import Enum

class PriorityLevel(Enum):
    HIGH = 1
    NORMAL = 2
    LOW = 3
    BATCH = 4

@dataclass
class CostModel:
    backend_id: str
    cost_per_shot: float
    volume_threshold: int
    volume_discount: float  # 0.0-1.0
    priority_multipliers: Dict[PriorityLevel, float]

@dataclass
class JobSpec:
    job_id: str
    shots: int
    priority: PriorityLevel

@dataclass
class CostEstimate:
    backend: str
    base_cost: float
    discounts: float
    final_cost: float
    cost_per_shot: float

class CostOptimizer:

exercises.expectedOutput: A cost optimizer that provides estimates and optimization suggestions
hints: Apply discounts in order: volume first, then priority multiplier
Sort suggestions by potential savings (highest first)
Batching combines shots and uses BATCH priority
Always return sorted comparison results by final cost

exercises.title: Implement a Resource Pool
exercises.description: Create a resource pooling system for managing shared access to quantum backends with automatic cleanup and reservation capabilities.
tasks: Define PooledResource dataclass to track resource state
Implement acquire and release operations with timeout handling
Add automatic cleanup of expired allocations
Create a context manager for safe resource usage
Implement capacity reservation for guaranteed access
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
from contextlib import contextmanager
import threading
import time

@dataclass
class PooledResource:
    resource_id: str
    backend_id: str
    allocated: bool = False
    allocated_to: Optional[str] = None
    allocated_at: Optional[float] = None

class ResourcePool:
    """Manages a pool of quantum resources."""
    
    def __init__(
        self,
        resources_per_backend: int = 5,
        allocation_timeout: float = 3600

exercises.expectedOutput: A thread-safe resource pool with automatic cleanup and reservation
hints: Use a lock to protect concurrent access to the pool
The context manager must always release in finally block
Cleanup should run before each acquire attempt
Reservation is just acquiring multiple resources at once
